color.description: Parses a color string (hex, rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla, or named color keyword) and returns a color value that can be used in further color manipulation functions. This is the base function for constructing color values programmatically in Less stylesheets.

darken.description: Decreases the lightness of the given color by the specified percentage amount in the HSL color space. The optional method parameter can be set to 'relative' for relative percentage changes instead of absolute. Useful for generating darker variants of a base color for hover states or borders.

lighten.description: Increases the lightness of the given color by the specified percentage amount in the HSL color space. Commonly used for creating lighter shades for backgrounds, disabled states, and accent variations from a primary theme color.

mix.description: Blends two colors together proportionally based on the weight parameter (defaulting to 50%). A weight of 25% mixes 25% of color1 with 75% of color2. Opacity is also factored into the weighting calculation for semi-transparent colors.

fade.description: Sets the absolute opacity of a color to the specified percentage amount (0% to 100%). Unlike fadein/fadeout which adjust opacity relatively, fade sets it to an exact value, making it predictable for creating semi-transparent overlay colors.

spin.description: Rotates the hue of a color by the given angle in degrees on the color wheel (0 to 360). Positive values rotate clockwise, negative counter-clockwise. Useful for generating complementary and analogous color schemes from a single base color.

percentage.description: Converts a unitless number to a percentage value by multiplying by 100. For example, percentage(0.5) returns 50%. Useful when computing relative dimensions or widths from ratios stored in variables.

unit.description: Changes or removes the unit of a dimension value. With one argument, strips the unit and returns a unitless number. With two arguments, replaces the unit with the specified one. Commonly used for unit conversions and arithmetic between incompatible unit types.

.mixin.description: Defines a reusable set of CSS declarations (a mixin) that can accept parameters with optional default values. Mixins are invoked by their selector name and can be included in any ruleset. They support pattern matching, guards (when conditions), and the ... rest argument syntax for variadic parameters.

@variable.description: Declares a Less variable that stores a value for reuse throughout the stylesheet. Variables can hold colors, dimensions, strings, or any CSS value and are evaluated lazily — meaning the last definition in scope wins. They enable centralized theming, consistent spacing scales, and easy global style changes.
